data.title: Hanover Insurance Group Inc reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:

The mean expectation of seven analysts for the quarter was for earnings of $4.19 per share. Wall Street expected results to range from $3.90 to $4.53 per share.

  • Hanover Insurance Group Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was $5.20​.

FORECAST CHANGES

data.title: Cigna's Earnings Could Show It Has Cleared Away PBM Surprises — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Catherine Dunn

The Cigna Group elicited plenty of questions from Wall Street analysts when it announced a big shift to its pharmacy benefits business model last fall, by planning to go "rebate free." But heading into its first-quarter earnings results on Thursday, the company may find it has done enough heavy lifting to explain the change.

That's in large part because, in February, Cigna's Express Scripts was the first of three major pharmacy-benefit managers, known as PBMs, to reach a settlement agreement with the Federal Trade Commission over insulin pricing litigation. No liability was admitted.

The settlement terms reflected the previously announced rebate-free model and helped Wall Street make better sense of the transition to a new way of generating revenue in a significant business line.

"We don't expect too many surprises with CI's 1Q26 earnings print, with likely inline cost trends / MLR on pricing cushions and updates on the progress so far with the company's rebate-free PBM model flip," said a preview note from Guggenheim Securities, referencing an acronym for the company's medical loss ratio.

Shares are up nearly 5% this year as of Wednesday's close.

Wall Street expects adjusted earnings per share of $7.60, up from $6.74 during the same quarter a year ago, according to FactSet.

The consensus estimate for quarterly revenue lands at $66.29 billion versus $65.45 billion the same period last year.

Cigna is in the middle of a CEO change, with current Chief Operating Officer Brian Evanko set to replace chief executive David Cordani when he retires July 1.

Write to Catherine Dunn at catherine.dunn@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: VICI lifts annual AFFO forecast on experiential real estate investments
data.description:
VICI Properties lifted its 2026 adjusted funds from operations forecast on Wednesday, banking on investments in experiential real estate to drive growth.

  • The company expects its full-year adjusted FFO per share to range between $2.44 and $2.47, up from its earlier projection of $2.42 to $2.45.

  • VICI's portfolio, which includes casinos such as Caesars Palace and MGM Grand in Las Vegas, positions it to benefit from growing consumer preference for experiences over goods.

  • Beyond casinos, the REIT has diversified into other leisure and entertainment assets, including bowling alleys, wellness resorts, indoor water parks and golf courses.

  • The hospitality and entertainment REIT reported adjusted FFO of 61 cents per share for the quarter ended March 31, missing analysts' average estimate of 66 cents per share.

  • Total revenue for the quarter stood at about $1.02 billion, a 3.5% increase from the same period last year.

  • Analysts, on average, expected quarterly revenue of $1.02 billion, according to data compiled by LSEG.

data.title: Bausch Health 1Q Loss Widens on $1.43B Goodwill Impairment Charge
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Bausch Health reported a $1.43 billion goodwill impairment charge related to Salix's RED-C program in its most recent quarter, widening the company's loss.

The Canadian pharmaceutical company on Wednesday recorded a loss of $1.42 billion, or $3.82 a share, compared with a loss of $58 million, or 16 cents a share, a year earlier. The loss was driven by the impairment charge, partially offset by a higher gross profit.

Stripping out certain one-time items, including the charge, the company reported adjusted earnings of 78 cents a share. Analysts polled by FactSet were expecting 67 cents a share.

Revenue rose to $2.52 billion, up from $2.26 billion a year prior. Analysts were expecting $2.41 billion in revenue.

Shares rose 3.4%, to $5.78, in after-hours trading, after closing down 1.4% at $5.59. Ahead of the close, the stock was down more than 20% year to date.

The company reaffirmed its full-year outlook for revenue between $10.67 billion and $10.92 billion, with adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization between $3.89 billion and $4.01 billion.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: MGM Resorts' quarterly profit misses estimates amid slower Las Vegas business
data.description:
MGM Resorts International missed Wall Street expectations for first-quarter profit on Thursday, hurt by weaker performance of its Las Vegas businesses amid ongoing macroeconomic uncertainty.

Falling visits to Las Vegas has led to a slump in demand for luxury vacations at the company's properties in the region, including resorts, hotels and casinos.

Revenue from its Las Vegas Strip Resorts came in at $2.2 billion for the first quarter, slightly higher than a year ago.

"We are seeing signs of strength driven by solid convention bookings, our newly launched all-inclusive promotion, and our recently refreshed rooms at the MGM Grand Las Vegas," CEO Bill Hornbuckle said.

Its quarterly adjusted per-share profit of 49 cents came below Wall Street expectations of 53 cents, according to data compiled by LSEG.

MGM's total revenue rose 4.1% to $4.45 billion in the first quarter ended March 31. Analysts, on average, estimated $4.37 billion.

Revenue from MGM China, representing the company's operations in China and Macau, grew 9% to $1.1 billion for the quarter.

data.title: The Hanover reports Q1 net income $186.8M; operating EPS $5.25; combined ratio 91.7%
data.description:
The Hanover reported first-quarter 2026 net income of $186.8 million, or $5.20 per diluted share, and operating income of $188.5 million, or $5.25 per diluted share. The company posted a combined ratio of 91.7% (85.4% excluding catastrophes) and record operating return on equity of 20.3%. Net premiums written rose 3.2% to $1,559.7 million and net investment income increased 19.6% to $126.9 million.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue (Premiums earned): $1,570.6 million for the three months ended March 31, 2026.
  • Operating income: $188.5 million; operating income per diluted share: $5.25.
  • Net income: $186.8 million; diluted EPS: $5.20.
  • Combined ratio: 91.7%; combined ratio excluding catastrophes: 85.4%.
  • Net investment income: $126.9 million (up 19.6% year-over-year).

Business Highlights

  • Net premiums written increased 3.2% year-over-year to $1,559.7 million, with growth across segments (Core Commercial +4.3%, Specialty +2.3%, Personal Lines +2.7%).
  • Renewal price increases: Core Commercial +8.6%, Personal Lines +8.4%, Specialty +4.6%; rate increases noted in Core Commercial (+7.5%), Personal Lines (+4.3%) and Specialty (+2.4%).
  • Segment performance: Specialty delivered strong profitability (combined ratio 84.2%); Core Commercial improved materially (combined ratio 96.6%) driven by lower property large losses and expense leverage; Personal Lines sustained margins with current-year combined ratio ex-CAT improving modestly.
  • Reserve development was favorable across segments, including meaningful prior‑year reserve benefit in Specialty and favorable catastrophe reserve development in multiple segments.
  • Capital actions: repurchased ~580,000 shares year-to-date through April 28, 2026 (~$101 million) and maintained book value per share of $101.86 (or $107.14 excluding unrealized fixed maturity depreciation).

Original SEC Filing: HANOVER INSURANCE GROUP, INC. [ THG ] - 8-K - Apr. 29, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Profusa Updates Financing Terms: Note Now Convertible; 19.99% Cap Added; Warrant Amended
data.description:
Profusa updated multiple financing instruments to enhance flexibility and align with Nasdaq rules. The company modified its promissory note with NorthView Sponsor I, keeping $1,869,796 outstanding, removing interest, extending maturity to Dec 31, 2026, and adding an equity conversion at the greater of 95% of the closing price or $0.35 per share, subject to a 4.99% cap. A subsequent amendment added a 19.99% exchange cap unless shareholder approval is obtained, with a commitment to seek such approval within 90 days and periodically thereafter. Separately, Profusa amended a warrant with Ascent Partners Fund to remove automatic conversion or assumption provisions in fundamental transactions, with all other warrant terms unchanged.

Agreement 1: Profusa Amends Note; $1.87M Outstanding, Convertible at 95% of Price or $0.35 Floor

  • Agreement type: Note Modification and Conversion Agreement
  • Counterparty: NorthView Sponsor I
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 24 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: Until Dec 31 2026
  • Reason: Extend maturity and enable equity conversion flexibility

Agreement 2: Profusa Adds 19.99% Exchange Cap, Will Seek Shareholder Approval Under Nasdaq Rules

  • Agreement type: Amendment No. 1 to Note Modification and Conversion Agreement
  • Counterparty: NorthView Sponsor I
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 29 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Comply with Nasdaq shareholder approval requirements

Agreement 3: Profusa Amends April 2026 Warrant, Removes Fundamental Transaction Conversion Terms

  • Agreement type: Amendment to Warrant to Purchase Shares of Common Stock
  • Counterparty: Ascent Partners Fund
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 29 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Adjust warrant terms for potential transactions

Original SEC Filing: Profusa, Inc. [ PFSA ] - 8-K - Apr. 29, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Profusa Disclosed Failure to Satisfy a Continued Listing Rule or Standard
data.description:
On April 28, 2026, the company received notice from Nasdaq that it had not regained compliance with Listing Rule 5450(b)(1)(C) requiring a $15 million market value of publicly held shares by the April 27, 2026 deadline. A Nasdaq Hearings Panel will consider this along with the company’s separate deficiencies under the $1.00 bid price and $50 million MVLS rules; the company may submit its views by May 5, 2026 and intends to respond, but there is no assurance of continued listing.

Original SEC Filing: Profusa, Inc. [ PFSA ] - 8-K - Apr. 29, 2026

data.title: Alector Stops Phase 2 Trial for Early Alzheimer's Treatment
data.description:
By Christopher Kuo

Alector is discontinuing its Phase 2 trial of its nivisnebart treatment for early Alzheimer's disease.

The biotechnology company said Wednesday that the Phase 2 PROGRESS-AD trial would be discontinued because it was unlikely to meet its goal of slowing disease progression at completion.

"This outcome is disappointing for patients and families affected by Alzheimer's disease and underscores the complexity of developing effective treatments for this devastating disease," Chief Executive Arnon Rosenthal said.

Alector said it had been co-developing nivisnebart with GSK.

The company remains focused on a variety of other programs to treat neurodegenerative diseases, it said.

Write to Christopher Kuo at chris.kuo@wsj.com
